name: LangAlpha
what_it_does_well: Closest product to the Aegis vision: 'Claude Code for investing' — tiered data ecosystem (Polygon→FMP→yfinance), ~23 finance skills, research-workbench front-end with provenance panel.
what_we_took: Data-tier architecture and programmatic tool-calling as reference; catalyst-calendar skill is the reference for the 'explain the 300% move' feature.
what_we_rejected: Their epistemics: no forward track record, no experiment registry, no 24-month discipline, no DSR/PBO gate — borrow their surface, keep our spine.
still_unmined: The mining pass (data-tier + catalyst-calendar) scheduled, not done.

name: anthropics/financial-services
what_it_does_well: Official Anthropic finance-domain agent patterns and skills; LangAlpha itself adapts from it.
what_we_took: Skill structure and prompt patterns for financial-analysis tasks (reference).
still_unmined: No concrete absorption recorded beyond reference status.

name: ML4T (stefan-jansen/machine-learning-for-trading)
what_it_does_well: The ML-for-trading reference text-as-repo; feature engineering, factor construction, alt-data handling.
what_we_took: Feature-engineering/factor-construction reference for the multi-factor work (T8).
what_we_rejected: Its backtesting teachings are governed by T7 — direction-checks only on our data; validation stays forward.

name: ECC (affaan-m/ECC)
what_it_does_well: Claude Code 'agent OS': continuous-learning 'instincts' pattern (auto-extract session patterns with confidence scoring) + memory-persistence hooks.
what_we_took: Named as the mature reference for what Optimus's postmortem→brain loop should become.
what_we_rejected: Wholesale install — sprawling and finicky; extract patterns.
still_unmined: ECC-style instinct extraction for Optimus.

name: thedotmack/claude-mem
what_it_does_well: A Claude memory system (retrieval, distillation cadence) used as a comparison point.
what_we_took: Ideas to compare against Optimus only.
what_we_rejected: Replacing Optimus — 'compare, don't replace'.
still_unmined: Comparison never recorded as done.

name: obra/superpowers + vercel-labs/skills
what_it_does_well: Claude Code skill/workflow pattern libraries; SKILL.md authoring pattern (frontmatter + imperative body).
what_we_took: Pattern re-implemented as the five Aegis discipline skills in .claude/skills/ (2026-07-08).
what_we_rejected: Vendoring any external framework.

name: farion1231/cc-switch
what_it_does_well: Model/provider switcher for Claude Code; tooling convenience only.
what_we_took: Nothing — nothing enters the repo.
what_we_rejected: Any repo adoption.

name: backtesting.py (kernc)
what_it_does_well: Clean single-file backtesting library — cleaner than hand-rolled loops for direction-check harnesses.
what_we_took: Nothing yet; candidate pip dependency.
what_we_rejected: Any alpha-grade use: a nicer backtester cannot fix a survivors-only universe (T7); output is direction-grade only.
still_unmined: Candidate-dependency status never exercised.

name: Microsoft Qlib
what_it_does_well: ~45k-star quant platform; Alpha158/Alpha360 factor formula library; best open-source PIT-database schema reference.
what_we_took: Verdict 'learn-from, keep our own loop' (2026-06-20); Alpha158 formulas flagged (MIT) for return models.
what_we_rejected: The framework: A-share origin, .bin format, adopting it means rebuilding our pipeline around its abstractions; US collectors broken 2026; inherits T7.
still_unmined: PIT-DB schema borrow (highest-leverage gap per SOURCES_USED) + Alpha158 formulas.

name: Microsoft RD-Agent / RD-Agent(Q)
what_it_does_well: Automated factor/model R&D loop with hypothesis memory — industrial cousin of lab/rd_loop.py; the one surveyed system with credible evidence (post-LLM-cutoff test windows).
what_we_took: Ranked absorb (2026-07-14): hypothesis memory + ratchet — factors admitted only on beating incumbent library, post-cutoff windows only.
what_we_rejected: Adopting qlib-the-platform it rides on.
still_unmined: The absorb itself — pattern-mine when lab loop next evolves.

name: Vibe-Trading (HKUDS)
what_it_does_well: NL→backtest in one agent loop; 30-loader IP-ban-ordered fallback registry; ~450-factor alpha zoo with per-factor metadata + mass IC bench; AST sandbox; 21.5k stars in 3.5 months.
what_we_took: Catch-up plan named: loader fallback chains → provider registry, alpha-zoo registration pattern, IC bench on FORWARD PIT snapshots, AST sandbox if lab executes generated code.
what_we_rejected: Its validation: statistically shallow — NO purged CV, NO DSR/PBO, NO survivorship handling, NO forward record; its alive/dead t>2 factor screen is a textbook data-mining machine.
still_unmined: All four catch-up items.

name: TradingAgents (TauricResearch)
what_it_does_well: Bull-vs-bear debate multi-agent framework; the debate + risk-team-veto prompt structure is the one part that survives adversarial review.
what_we_took: Debate-as-explanation: bull/bear two-sided card shipped 2026-07-17 (commit 4ded5bb; LLM argues both sides of the COMPUTED signal, numeric signal untouched, advice language fail-closed).
what_we_rejected: Framework + trading premise: backtest is ~3 months of mega-caps inside the LLM training cutoff; agents degrade under live conditions (Agent Market Arena).
still_unmined: Debate prompt for conviction-lane decision logging.

name: ai-hedge-fund (virattt)
what_it_does_well: ~43k-star LLM investor-persona stock-picking (Buffett/Burry/Wood agents → PM agent); entertaining, educational.
what_we_took: Nothing — nothing here Aegis lacks.
what_we_rejected: Everything: no live evidence; one independent test found short-horizon Sharpe -1.43; persona ensembles are vocabulary, not skill.

name: FinRL / FinRL-Meta (AI4Finance)
what_it_does_well: Deep-RL allocation framework whose authors honestly document endemic backtest overfitting (propose rejecting agents with >10% overfit probability).
what_we_took: Confidence only: their overfit-rejection gate confirms our DSR/PBO discipline is the industrial direction, not paranoia.
what_we_rejected: The framework and RL-trading premise: no live record, backtest-only, self-criticized. Also CANON §4: no RL on P&L.

name: FinGPT (AI4Finance)
what_it_does_well: FinGPT-Forecaster's structured prompt contract (what happened / impact / risks to watch) and FPB/FiQA sentiment benchmarks.
what_we_took: Forecaster prompt contract absorbed into the daily brief (/api/news/brief, 2026-07-14, descriptive-only enforced).
what_we_rejected: LoRA fine-tuning and any LLM-as-stock-picker; sentiment backtests are backtest-only.
still_unmined: FinBERT validation vs FPB/FiQA benchmarks (backlog).

name: FinMem
what_it_does_well: Layered memory with class-specific decay and temporal embargo for LLM agents.
what_we_took: Ranked absorb (2026-07-14): layered memory + temporal embargo for news/brain retrieval — embargo inoculates against the field's #1 bug.
what_we_rejected: The LLM-trader premise (F-009).
still_unmined: The memory pattern itself — not built.

name: FinCon
what_it_does_well: NeurIPS 2024: risk-triggered reflection — drawdown/CVaR breach triggers structured self-critique.
what_we_took: Ranked absorb: lane drawdown/CVaR breach → structured postmortem (market-error vs decision-error split) → brain context; never touches the NAV write-path.
what_we_rejected: Any LLM near the trade path.
still_unmined: The reflection loop — not built.

name: StockSharp
what_it_does_well: Trading-platform core (C#).
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: The core — no crash-prediction or validation edge over what Aegis runs (2026-07-14 12-repo scan).

name: vnpy
what_it_does_well: Chinese trading-framework core.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Same blanket rejection as StockSharp; no edge over Aegis.

name: Qbot (UFund-Me)
what_it_does_well: AI quant-trading bot.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: License: CC BY-NC-SA is incompatible with MIT Aegis.

name: hyperswitch
what_it_does_well: Payments infrastructure — appeared in the 12-repo survey; irrelevant to a market-intelligence engine.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Out of domain entirely.

name: myhhub/stock
what_it_does_well: Named in session prompts but no recorded examination found in docs; pitch is stock selection/prediction — the rejected-by-category class.
what_we_rejected: Pre-emptively by category: predicts-prices pitch (CANON §7, F-001/F-002 class) — do not re-test.

name: ZhuLinsen/daily_stock_analysis
what_it_does_well: Daily-brief pipeline shape: one batched market tape + holdings-relevant headlines + LLM summary.
what_we_took: Pipeline shape shipped 2026-07-14 as the daily brief (GET /api/news/brief + dashboard card), spend-guarded with deterministic template fallback.

name: Fincept Terminal
what_it_does_well: Geopolitical shock surface concept.
what_we_took: Concept only — zero code.
what_we_rejected: All code (AGPL + damages clause).
still_unmined: The geopolitical shock surface concept.

name: FinanceDatabase (JerBouma)
what_it_does_well: Universe metadata catalog (tickers, sectors, categories).
what_we_took: Nothing yet; ranked-backlog item for universe metadata.
still_unmined: The universe-metadata integration.

name: OpenBB
what_it_does_well: 'Connect once, consume everywhere' data platform; Open Data Platform provider-standardization direction worth watching against our provider registry.
what_we_took: Nothing — no gap.
what_we_rejected: Code (house rule: AGPL → no code, ever) and backtesting through its live calls (no PIT control; most providers need their own paid keys).
still_unmined: ODP standardization ideas — watch only.

name: gs-quant (Goldman Sachs)
what_it_does_well: gs_quant.timeseries measures work without a GS account — reference implementations for vol/tail analytics.
what_we_took: Nothing yet; ranked absorb: cross-check our vol/tail analytics against theirs.
what_we_rejected: Everything Marquee-gated.
still_unmined: The cross-check pass.

name: skfolio
what_it_does_well: Very active portfolio-ML library: portfolio-level CombinatorialPurgedCV + WalkForward, tested Denoise/DetoneCovariance, VineCopula synthetic stress, EntropyPooling.
what_we_took: Nothing yet; verdict 'migrate opportunistically, never big-bang' (could replace hand-rolled Marchenko-Pastur).
what_we_rejected: Big-bang migration.
still_unmined: CPCV-for-allocation, Denoise/Detone replacement, VineCopula stress.

name: vectorbt
what_it_does_well: Very fast vectorized backtests/param sweeps.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: SKIP: faster backtests are misaligned with forward-only validation (T7); Commons Clause adds license overhead; vectorized = easy lookahead.
still_unmined: Revisit only if lab sweeps become a bottleneck.

name: PyBroker
what_it_does_well: Bootstrap-CI pattern: BCa confidence intervals on strategy metrics.
what_we_took: The TECHNIQUE, not the framework: BCa bootstrap CIs on every lane's Sharpe/Sortino/maxDD, shipped 2026-07-17 (/api/pi/lane/{id}/stats-ci, commit afe62ec).
what_we_rejected: The framework (Commons Clause).
still_unmined: Block bootstrap for Sharpe/Sortino (iid bootstrap understates width — V4_CLOSEOUT lack #3).

name: quantstats
what_it_does_well: One-call HTML tearsheets (Sharpe/Sortino/drawdown/underwater/monthly heatmap) from a return series.
what_we_took: Lane tearsheets at /api/pi/lane/{id}/tearsheet (2026-07-17, commit afe62ec).
what_we_rejected: Its yfinance fetch utilities (we have the PIT layer).

name: alphalens (alphalens-reloaded)
what_it_does_well: IC analysis: IC decay, quantile monotonicity, turnover — the bench every signal must pass before it earns a lane.
what_we_took: Concept hand-rolled in engine/validation/factor_ic.py (deliberately no dependency) grading T8/T9/T10 forward signals; package installed but zero importers.
what_we_rejected: Direct dependence on the critical path.
still_unmined: Run alphalens-reloaded on existing PIT forward snapshots for publication-ready IC decay/quantile/turnover plots.

name: zipline-reloaded
what_it_does_well: The Pipeline API (rank a universe → top-N) — exactly our missing cross-sectional ranker.
what_we_took: Pipeline pattern reimplemented in backend/services/cross_sectional_momentum.py (percentile rank within universe).
what_we_rejected: The bundle/event system — our own PIT-safe event loop stays source of truth.
still_unmined: Production-grade ranker→top-N→constructor→Riskfolio flow.

name: NautilusTrader
what_it_does_well: Research-to-live code parity is the gold standard if we ever go live; Rust core, bi-weekly releases.
what_we_took: Nothing — reference, not adopt now (paper-only project).
what_we_rejected: Adoption now; parity is code-level, not a guarantee backtest = live results.
still_unmined: Later live-bridge reference.

name: backtrader
what_it_does_well: Once-standard Python backtester.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Dead since ~2018; own PIT-safe loop stays source of truth.

name: bt (pmorissette)
what_it_does_well: Fine for quick multi-asset monthly-rebalance prototyping.
what_we_took: Nothing — not installed.
what_we_rejected: As source of truth; acceptable only for throwaway prototyping, which we don't need.

name: QuantConnect / LEAN
what_it_does_well: Survivorship-free 1998+ cloud data with shareable result URLs; their Alpha Streams shutdown postmortem ('selection results in strong overfitting') externally validates the house doctrine.
what_we_took: Postmortem cited as external validation; ranked absorb: free-cloud re-run of 2-3 lane mandates as third-party attestation.
what_we_rejected: LEAN as source of truth (C#, cloud-coupled).
still_unmined: The QC free-cloud attestation runs.

name: Lumibot
what_it_does_well: Broker-execution framework.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: No audited community results found; live results unverifiable.

name: freqtrade
what_it_does_well: Honest-by-abstention: publishes no performance claims; docs actively warn public strategies underperform and hyperopt overfits.
what_we_took: The warnings-at-the-point-of-temptation pattern (overfitting warnings inside the hyperopt docs) — worth applying to any future lane-config surface.
what_we_rejected: Community '2,509%' return posts (unaudited backtests); crypto harness not needed.
still_unmined: Point-of-temptation warnings on lane-config surfaces.

name: Composer.trade
what_it_does_well: Best UI pattern in the space: public symphonies show out-of-sample stats accrued AFTER creation date — creation-date-stamped OOS accounting.
what_we_took: Pattern earmarked for the track-record page: surface since-pre-registration as the only headline number.
what_we_rejected: Aggregate live-vs-backtest stats (unverifiable).
still_unmined: The since-pre-registration headline treatment.

name: NexusTrade
what_it_does_well: Founder put $25k real money public (Feb 2026) — a transparency commitment; weak-but-honest (prospective).
what_we_took: Nothing; results not matured.
what_we_rejected: Closed-source LLM-trading platform premise.

name: eToro / Wikifolio (social copy-trading)
what_it_does_well: Nothing for followers: copy-trading causes excessive risk-taking (Apesteguia et al. 2020); wikifolio ~1,084 portfolios show no alpha on average.
what_we_took: The negative evidence as a prior for all follower features.
what_we_rejected: Social copy-trading entirely — followers don't reliably profit.

name: NANC/KRUZ + Autopilot + Unusual Whales
what_it_does_well: Marketing, not edge: NANC beat SPY raw but shows no risk-adjusted outperformance; Unusual Whales' own report: only 32.2% of Congress beat SPY.
what_we_took: Evidence matched TRIAL-CONGRESS-IC's frozen weak-to-null prior exactly.
what_we_rejected: A congress-follower lane (the edge is dead in the data; the products are beta + story).

name: Zacks
what_it_does_well: Nothing verified: 24%/yr = hypothetical equal-weight frictionless churn, their own disclosure concedes it.
what_we_took: Cited to debunk analyst-ratings-level products; supports revisions-not-levels (T10).
what_we_rejected: Rank-style ratings products as evidence of edge.

name: DeepFund (arena)
what_it_does_well: Leakage-controlled LIVE LLM-trading arena: live month showed 8 of 9 flagship LLMs net-negative.
what_we_took: Core evidence for F-009: the forward record + pre-registration IS the product moat; absorb components, never LLM-as-trader.

name: Alpha Arena (nof1.ai)
what_it_does_well: Real-money live LLM trading: S1 GPT-5 -62.7%, Gemini 2.5 Pro -56.7%, Claude Sonnet 4.5 -30.8%; only DeepSeek and Qwen3 Max positive (fact-checked 2026-07-16).
what_we_took: F-009 evidence that frontier LLMs lose money trading live; moat confirmation.

name: LiveTradeBench + Agent Market Arena
what_it_does_well: 21 LLMs traded live 50 days — LMArena rank does NOT predict trading outcome; framework matters more than model; agents degrade under live conditions.
what_we_took: External validation of the house doctrine: backtest selection ≈ overfitting selection; live degradation is the norm.

name: Metaculus
what_it_does_well: Scores against explicit baselines (chance/peers) with calibration plots and dotted zero-skill reference lines on every chart — the honest-uncertainty display gold standard.
what_we_took: The researched playbook for the track-record page (F-019): baseline reference lines, 'too early' as first-class state, frequency framing, no 1%-precision.
still_unmined: The entire playbook — researched, not applied (V5 item 2).

name: Betterment
what_it_does_well: Downside-weighted allocation recommendation (max average outcome across 5th-50th pct band); labeled risk-deviation slider; exact plain-English band sentences on its P10/P50/P90 fan.
what_we_took: Cheap absorbs identified (F-017): band-labeling sentences on the existing MC fan, probability-of-target with three levers.
what_we_rejected: The binary On-Track badge and auto-adjustment — where education crosses into advice.
still_unmined: All F-017 absorbs (V5 item 5).

name: Wealthfront
what_it_does_well: Combines subjective+objective risk weighting the more risk-averse component (overconfidence mitigation); penalizes inconsistent answers.
what_we_took: Absorbs identified (F-017): conservative-component weighting; stated-risk-vs-horizon contradiction flag (matches SEC IM Guidance 2017-02).
what_we_rejected: Auto-adjustment/advice-crossing features.
still_unmined: All F-017 absorbs (V5 item 5).

name: Portfolio Visualizer
what_it_does_well: The reference factor-lens presentation: loadings with t-stats/p-values/R² and rolling windows — but paywalled its community out ($30-55/mo) and discloses survivorship bias nowhere.
what_we_took: Absorbs identified (F-018): loadings-with-statistics presentation on the existing FF5+MOM engine; publish our T7 negative result loudly — beats both incumbents' silence.
what_we_rejected: Their silence on survivorship.
still_unmined: Factor-lens presentation — the single clearest product differentiator available (V5 item 3).

name: testfol.io
what_it_does_well: Captured PV's priced-out community; factor-return contribution column (loading × premium); proxy-backfilled long histories with published recipes. Now tiering too; also silent on survivorship.
what_we_took: Absorbs identified (F-018): contribution column, proxy-backfill recipes, per-source data table.
what_we_rejected: Survivorship silence.
still_unmined: All F-018 absorbs.

name: Ghostfolio
what_it_does_well: Mature open-source portfolio tracker that — even WITH a database — chose CSV/manual import over aggregators; proven import pattern (header-alias map, row validation, preview-before-commit).
what_we_took: The CSV-import pattern documented as the ~1-day path if holdings import is ever wanted (client-side papaparse; holdings never leave the browser).
what_we_rejected: Copying AGPL code (reimplement the pattern); as a product: no pre-registered forward records.
still_unmined: The CSV import itself.

name: SnapTrade
what_it_does_well: Brokerage aggregation API; verified $1-2/connected-user/month.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Architecturally closed to Aegis: per-user server-side secrets = accounts + a database, both design-forbidden (F-020).

name: Plaid
what_it_does_well: Bank/brokerage aggregation; 10 lifetime trial Items then contract pricing.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Same F-020 grounds: per-user access_token = accounts + DB (design-forbidden), compliance-gated.

name: WorldMonitor
what_it_does_well: Named in early competitive-analysis memory; no recorded examination in research docs.
still_unmined: Entire project (low priority — no identified gap it fills).

name: fatcrash
what_it_does_well: Event-window crash-evaluation harness (406 crash / 1,061 non-crash windows, 37+ assets); its negative results (LPPLS top F1 ≈ 0.48) corroborate our descriptive-only LPPLS stance.
what_we_took: Borrow-pattern plan: eval harness + estimator menu (Taleb kappa, Hill tail index, critical slowing down), clean-room reimplementation only.
what_we_rejected: Any code copy — no license file, contamination risk.
still_unmined: The clean-room harness (crash plan P1).

name: jumpmodels (Shu/Mulvey)
what_it_does_well: Statistical jump models for regime ID — more label-stable than HMM, explicitly online/causal inference API (predict_proba_online) avoiding the leakage trap our HMM path shares.
what_we_took: Named 'the one item this scan would actually install' — candidate pip dependency for regime features.
still_unmined: Installation and wiring.

name: Open Source Asset Pricing (Chen-Zimmermann)
what_it_does_well: 212 peer-reviewed cross-sectional predictors + portfolio returns through Dec 2024, free, no WRDS needed; actively maintained (Python release Oct 2025, pip install openassetpricing).
what_we_took: Planned as direction-check sanity harness for T8/T12 ('CZ harness'); 2026-07-17 sweep re-ranked it leverage #1: a ready-made menu of pre-registerable forward-IC trials with published priors — the pit_score_collector engine already generalizes to them.
what_we_rejected: Vendoring GPL code (data downloads are the asset); using it as an alpha claim for our implementations.
still_unmined: Register 2-3 OSAP predictors buildable on free data as forward-IC trials; CZ direction-check harness.

name: JKP Global Factor Data (Jensen-Kelly-Pedersen)
what_it_does_well: 153 factors, 13 themes, 93 countries, free download; JF 2023 'Is There a Replication Crisis in Finance?' methodology; the benchmark for whether our composite adds anything over published factors.
what_we_took: Nothing yet — surfaced by the 2026-07-17 sweep.
still_unmined: Use factor returns as forward-IC comparison benchmark + citable methodology for the research paper. CC BY-NC fits a free educational platform (attribute, don't resell).

name: Hou-Xue-Zhang q-factor data
what_it_does_well: q5 factor returns 1967-2024 at daily/weekly/monthly + testing portfolios; RFS/JF citations.
what_we_took: Nothing yet — surfaced by the 2026-07-17 sweep.
still_unmined: Second independent academic factor benchmark alongside FF5/JKP for the paper.

name: WRDS student access (CRSP)
what_it_does_well: The only genuinely survivorship-free equity universe on a $0 budget — CRSP delisting returns. Undergraduate accounts are free at subscribing institutions; HKU subscribes.
what_we_took: Nothing yet. Earlier 'CRSP is out per Murat (eligibility + academic-only license)' (2026-07-16) predates the verified fact that undergrad accounts are free at subscribing schools.
what_we_rejected: Serving anything CRSP-derived from the public API (license). Offline research/certification only.
still_unmined: Murat: check HKU library portal for WRDS access. If granted: offline survivorship-free certification of lane mandates + the research paper's backtest section — partially unblocks T7 without overturning the free-data rule.

name: fja05680/sp500 (PIT S&P constituents)
what_it_does_well: S&P 500 membership 1996→present as CSV, manually curated (~2,595 date rows); free point-in-time index membership — kills the universe-SELECTION half of survivorship bias even where delisted prices stay missing.
what_we_took: Nothing yet — surfaced by the 2026-07-17 sweep. MIT = data directly usable.
still_unmined: Load as PIT universe table; combine with EODHD delisted prices (phase 2) for the full survivorship fix. Accuracy good post-2000, degrades earlier.

name: Kitces probability-of-adjustment framing
what_it_does_well: Research-backed replacement for binary success/failure MC display: 'X% chance of modest mid-course adjustment' + guardrail triggers/magnitudes; companion articles give citable design rationale.
what_we_took: Nothing yet — surfaced by the 2026-07-17 sweep.
still_unmined: Drop-in reframe for the retirement MC + SWR pages; pure frontend, zero data risk.

name: AIDE (WecoAI/aideml)
what_it_does_well: Cleanest open memory mechanism in the autonomous-research family: every experiment is a node in a persistent solution tree; metric feedback prunes branches; tree serialized per run. Cited by OpenAI/METR/Sakana.
what_we_took: Nothing yet — surfaced by the 2026-07-17 sweep.
still_unmined: Upgrade lab/ hypothesis memory from linear log to tree-with-ratchet (never revisit pruned branches, always branch from best node).

name: Agent Laboratory + AgentRxiv (Schmidgall)
what_it_does_well: Per-phase state_saves checkpointing + AgentRxiv shared 'preprint server' as cross-run memory: successive autonomous runs cite and build on prior runs' outputs.
what_we_took: Nothing yet — surfaced by the 2026-07-17 sweep.
still_unmined: The cross-run preprint-ledger layer for lab cycles (AIDE covers the within-run layer).

name: Quantile dotplots (Kay/Hullman/Fernandes)
what_it_does_well: Lab-validated frequency-framed uncertainty display ('count 2 dots in 20') — strongest published alternative to fan charts for a casual audience; improved real transit decisions in controlled studies.
what_we_took: Nothing yet — surfaced by the 2026-07-17 sweep; complements the F-019 frequency-framing playbook.
still_unmined: Re-implement in Recharts for MC goal pages (convert percentiles into countable outcomes).

name: pypbo (esvhd)
what_it_does_well: PBO/CSCV implementation.
what_we_took: Nothing — Aegis already has PSR/DSR/PBO wired in engine/validation/overfitting.py.
what_we_rejected: AGPL (incompatible even for heavy borrowing) + inactive. At most read its pytest cases for test ideas.

name: timeseriescv / TSCV (purged-CV libs)
what_it_does_well: Purged/embargoed CV classes.
what_we_took: Nothing — engine/validation/purged_cv.py already implements this.
what_we_rejected: Both effectively dormant; nothing to steal beyond what exists.

name: skfin (schampon)
what_it_does_well: Educational notebook book on ML-for-finance.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Backtester simpler than what's shipped; 38 stars, teaching material.

name: getfactormodels
what_it_does_well: Convenient multi-model factor fetcher (FF, q, AQR, ICR).
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Pre-alpha, license unclear; fetching the CSVs directly is trivial.

name: Databento (US equities)
what_it_does_well: High-quality market data with usage pricing.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: $199/mo standard plan — over budget; equities history shallow for survivorship work.

name: FinancialData.net
what_it_does_well: Breadth-first REST API with free tier.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Delisted-price depth and license terms not clearly documented; nothing beats Tiingo/Sharadar in this slot.

name: Sharadar SEP/SF1 (Nasdaq Data Link)
what_it_does_well: The one source that actually flips DIRECTIONAL → SIZING: delisted-inclusive prices since 1998 + as-reported PIT fundamentals (datekey) + PIT index membership.
what_we_took: Pre-registered SIZING in SOURCE_GUARANTEES with a documented one-session adapter path; nothing bought.
what_we_rejected: Buying now — demoted to fallback behind EODHD (2026-07-16). 2026-07-17 sweep: pricing is login-gated, could not verify <$25/mo.
still_unmined: One attended login to check the non-professional tier price — if ≤$25/mo it jumps to top-3 of the data shortlist.

name: Norgate Data
what_it_does_well: Best-in-class delisted coverage + historical index constituents to 1950 (Platinum).
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Verified $630/yr Platinum, no monthly billing — over budget ~2x; Windows-app-centric.

name: Tiingo
what_it_does_well: 30+ yr OHLCV across ~109k securities, free tier 500 symbols/mo; delisted coverage anecdotally decent but NOT formally documented.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Weak third option: ticker-rename handling reported messy; Power tier $30/mo (over the $25 line monthly); license bars serving its data from the public API anyway.
still_unmined: Only if EODHD phase 2 fails: verify delisted coverage empirically on the T7 name list before paying.

name: Stooq
what_it_does_well: Free bulk daily OHLCV, no API key, broad global coverage.
what_we_took: Nothing — shelf item (optional QA cross-check vs yfinance).
what_we_rejected: As a survivorship fix (would silently re-introduce survivorship bias under a new name); unreachable in our environment during the T7 audit; no stated reuse license.
still_unmined: The QA cross-check, if ever wanted.

name: EODHD
what_it_does_well: Cheapest credible survivorship-free feed: $19.99/mo, 26,000+ US delisted tickers mostly from Jan 2000; phase-1 acceptance PASSED 2026-07-16 (16/20 T7 delisted names present).
what_we_took: Phase-1 acceptance harness (engine/research/eodhd_acceptance.py).
what_we_rejected: No delisting-return methodology documented (phase-2 audit is the truth); HARD RULE: data stays OFFLINE in engine/research, nothing EODHD-derived served publicly.
still_unmined: Phase 2 (paid month) blocked on Murat's $19.99 subscription — the cheapest unblock in the whole validation program; then loader → survivorship rerun → PIT delisted panel → TRIAL-NN-1 draft.

name: CRSP via institutional WRDS (full license)
what_it_does_well: The institutional gold-standard survivorship-free panel.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Full institutional route out per Murat (eligibility + academic-only license); the STUDENT-account route is tracked separately (see 'WRDS student access').

name: kadoa-org/congress-trading-monitor
what_it_does_well: Independent congressional-trades dataset (54k+ transactions, 2012-present) from official disclosures.
what_we_took: Flagged as cross-check for TRIAL-CONGRESS-IC's FMP feed — validation source only, never primary. (senate/house-stock-watcher dumps found DEAD since 2021 and rejected.)
what_we_rejected: Primary-source use.
still_unmined: The cross-check wiring.

name: Quiver Quantitative
what_it_does_well: Candidate congressional-trading API; never deeply examined — T11 verified and chose FMP's free feed instead.
what_we_took: Nothing.
still_unmined: Head-to-head vs FMP only if FMP's congress feed degrades.

name: Capitol Trades
what_it_does_well: Congressional-disclosure source (30-45 day legal lag → descriptive only); never deeply examined — FMP chosen for T11.
what_we_took: Nothing.
still_unmined: Same fallback slot as Quiver.

name: AlgoVault
what_it_does_well: Crypto Merkle-proof track-record idea — nearest miss to our spine; nobody found does pre-registered paper-lane forward records.
what_we_took: Moat confirmation only.
what_we_rejected: Adoption.

name: hermes-agent / intelligent-trading-bot / investing-bot
what_it_does_well: LLM-agent and ML-predict-then-trade architectures (agent loops, feature pipelines).
what_we_took: Architecture ideas only, gated through pre-registered-forward-trial discipline.
what_we_rejected: Their core premise: 'agent learns from backtests to trade better' is precisely the backtest-to-alpha loop Aegis disproved (CANON §2-§4).

name: awesome-ai-in-finance (georgezouq)
what_it_does_well: Discovery index for OSS scans; nothing to adopt directly.
what_we_took: Browse/discovery source only.
what_we_rejected: Direct adoption of anything.

name: mlfinlab (Hudson & Thames)
what_it_does_well: Lopez de Prado method library: purged CV, triple-barrier labels, fractional differentiation, CPCV.
what_we_took: Concepts reimplemented from the primary papers: PSR/DSR/PBO-CSCV/CPCV/HLZ hurdle in engine/validation/overfitting.py; fracdiff/labeling/sample-uniqueness in engine/training/; purged CV in engine/validation/purged_cv.py.
what_we_rejected: Direct dependency (license/maintenance unattractive).

name: PyPortfolioOpt
what_it_does_well: Black-Litterman, HRP, Ledoit-Wolf covariance shrinkage.
what_we_took: Installed and used in portfolio_engine (BL + HRP).

name: Riskfolio-Lib
what_it_does_well: Stronger than PyPortfolioOpt for cross-asset CVaR / risk-parity / hierarchical budgeting.
what_we_took: Adopted: portfolio_optimizer.py + mpc_optimizer.py (Mean-CVaR, HRP, Risk Parity, Max Diversification).
still_unmined: Regime-rotator use.

name: hmmlearn
what_it_does_well: HMM regime classification (bull/bear/volatile/neutral).
what_we_took: Adopted in backend/models/hmm.py.
still_unmined: Leakage-safe online inference path + calibrated state probabilities (the HMM path shares the causal-inference leakage trap — jumpmodels is the candidate fix).

name: Autoresearch (reference repo)
what_it_does_well: Autonomous experiment loop with a 3-file contract and ratchet pattern.
what_we_took: The experiment-loop ratchet pattern for lab/rd_loop.py; read-only reference at C:/Users/mrthn/reference-codes/autoresearch.

name: FinBERT (ProsusAI)
what_it_does_well: Finance-tuned sentiment model; cheaper and better-validated for sentiment than a chat LLM.
what_we_took: Adopted in sentiment_analyzer.py (keyword fallback, kill-switch AEGIS_DISABLE_FINBERT, idle-unload RAM lever).
what_we_rejected: Replacing it with a chat LLM (would cost more and validate worse).
still_unmined: FPB/FiQA benchmark validation (backlog).

name: DeepSeek
what_it_does_well: Cheapest capable model for bulk text→data work; validated-JSON output contract with template fallback.
what_we_took: Adopted for news→structured JSON, brief summaries, commentary (llm_analyzer with spend guards); firewalled from all trade paths.
what_we_rejected: Fine-tuning and LLM-as-stock-picker.

name: yfinance (Yahoo Finance)
what_it_does_well: Free breadth: prices, financials, options, upgrades/downgrades — the primary price layer.
what_we_took: Core price layer (shared per-ticker fetch after F-015: single 10y history sliced per period, 90s breaker, stale-serve, 429→503 honesty); T10 revisions from .upgrades_downgrades. 2026-07-17: FRED DGS10/DGS3MO/DGS30 fallback added for treasury tickers (^TNX was rate-limited at boot).
what_we_rejected: As backtest universe: T7 proved 1/20 delisted names usable (F-004); graded DIRECTIONAL, never sizing. Chronic operational fragility (401 crumb storms, index-ticker rate limits) makes every new yfinance dependency a liability — prefer FRED/Alpaca/SEC where they overlap.
still_unmined: Migrate remaining fragile index tickers (daily_brief, world_markets ^TNX refs) to FRED equivalents.

name: FRED / fredapi
what_it_does_well: Free, deep macro time-series with no survivorship problem; several genuinely leading indicators; utterly reliable operationally (0 failed series in prod).
what_we_took: ~23 series wired in config.py; economic calendar card (V4); 2026-07-17: treasury-yield fallback (DGS10/DGS3MO/DGS30) for yfinance index tickers.
what_we_rejected: Treating revised series as PIT for backtests — truly PIT macro requires ALFRED vintages.
still_unmined: ALFRED first-release vintages; credit-quality term-structure adds (BBB/CCC/EM OAS, ANFCI/STLFSI4) as fragility candidates.

name: SEC EDGAR
what_it_does_well: The cleanest free alt-data Aegis can add — PIT by construction (filings stamped with filing date).
what_we_took: 13F collector, Form-4 insider collector (T9, via the _sec_get ≤8/s choke-point after the prod-403 incident F-005), fundamentals/Piotroski.
what_we_rejected: Raw unpaced fetching (100% prod failure while tests were green).
still_unmined: 8-K event mining, 10-K/10-Q risk-factor diffs, guidance/MD&A language mining — DATA_OPTIONS's #1 free recommendation.

name: edgartools
what_it_does_well: Cleanest EDGAR library: Form 3/4/5, XBRL 10-K/10-Q, 8-K full text, N-PORT parsing.
what_we_took: Adopted for the low-volume structured path (fundamentals.py); N-PORT schema flagged as reference for a T12 collector.
what_we_rejected: As runtime dep on the high-volume Form-4/Archives path — 50-min hang (T9) + the prod-403 incident; that path is hand-rolled through _sec_get.
still_unmined: N-PORT collector; 8-K text mining.

name: GDELT
what_it_does_well: Free global news/event scoring for the news-intelligence and geopolitical read.
what_we_took: Adopted in news_intelligence.py + daily brief; failed-fetch-is-disclosed-never-defaulted-to-calm rule (F-015); stale-serve honesty (V4). 2026-07-17: 1h result cache + failure cooldown + retry/stale-serve logs demoted to INFO — the perpetual 429 storm was self-inflicted warm-loop cadence.
what_we_rejected: Silent defaults on failure; WARNING-level logging of routine rate-limit churn (it buried real warnings in the 50-slot health buffer).

name: Google Trends / pytrends
what_it_does_well: Free fear/greed attention proxy; real for retail-driven/crypto names, noisy for large caps.
what_we_took: Adopted in trends_sentiment.py with 6h-cooldown honesty (one warning per outage, fast-fail after, None-disclosed downstream). Verdict 2026-07-17: keep as-is — Google blocks datacenter IPs so prod availability is inherently spotty, and the signal is optional/None-guarded everywhere.
what_we_rejected: Broad use beyond the niche contrarian tilt.

name: Polygon.io
what_it_does_well: Real-time quotes and intraday bars on a free tier.
what_we_took: Adopted: backend/services/polygon_client.py (real-time price snapshots).

name: Kenneth French Data Library
what_it_does_well: Canonical free factor-return series (FF5 + Momentum).
what_we_took: Adopted: factor_model.py FF5 decomposition + FF5+MOM 6-factor model; substrate for the planned F-018 factor lens.
still_unmined: Presentation layer with t-stats/rolling loadings/contribution column (V5 item 3).

name: Finnhub
what_it_does_well: Free-tier fundamentals/insider/ESG endpoints.
what_we_took: Adopted in insider_trading.py + esg.py (partial coverage).
what_we_rejected: As the T9 insider forward-IC source — missing fields; SEC Form 4 direct chosen instead.

name: FMP (Financial Modeling Prep)
what_it_does_well: Free tier serves congressional trades with disclosureDate + transactionDate (verified live) and works as a fallback provider for prices/fundamentals/estimates.
what_we_took: Adopted: TRIAL-CONGRESS-IC collector + fallback provider/comps/ESG; F-016 fixes (07:30 ET slot, APIKeys.redact()). 2026-07-17: fmp_budget.py daily-quota ledger — non-priority callers stop at budget-reserve, congress collector draws the reserved slice, live 402 fast-fails everyone (the 07:30 slot alone did NOT survive overnight fallback burn).
what_we_rejected: Treating a shared 250/day quota as unmetered.
still_unmined: Key rotation pending (Murat — leaked to Railway logs 07-16). delisted-companies endpoint as identity complement to fja05680 PIT membership.

name: Alpaca (paper API + IEX data)
what_it_does_well: Free paper accounts with third-party-computed NAV (Portfolio History API) — the single biggest trust upgrade available; free IEX-feed market data works as a secondary daily-bar source.
what_we_took: Paper-account mirror BUILT + deployed 2026-07-16 (commit 1803d95): never-reset policy, divergence monitor. 2026-07-17: keys validated live — account PA32NSWMCJ6P ACTIVE, $100k, zero positions, awaiting seed; IEX daily-bar endpoint verified working (yfinance-fallback candidate).
still_unmined: SEED: Murat sets ALPACA_API_KEY_ID/SECRET on Railway + one boot with AEGIS_SEED_ALPACA_MIRROR=1, then unset + rotate keys (passed through chat twice). Later: Alpaca IEX bars as a provider-registry fallback ahead of FMP for equity_history.

name: DoltHub stock datasets
what_it_does_well: Versioned-SQL data-sharing platform.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: 2026-07-17 sweep found no maintained delisted-equity database there; no verified asset.

name: CFTC Commitments of Traders
what_it_does_well: 40 years of institutional futures positioning (commercials/large specs/small traders): Legacy 1986+, disaggregated 2009+, TFF financial futures 2010+; free zipped CSVs, no registration. Dual-dated by design (Tuesday as-of vs Friday release) — PIT-gold.
what_we_took: Nothing yet — top-ranked find of the 2026-07-18 investor-history sweep; fits the generic pit_score_collector without new architecture.
still_unmined: A COT positioning collector (e.g. S&P/Treasury/gold net-spec extremes as descriptive fragility candidates); any signal use must be pre-registered forward.

name: Shiller data (shillerdata.com)
what_it_does_well: Monthly 1871→present: prices, dividends, earnings, CPI, long rate, CAPE — the only FREE MONTHLY 150-year series found; bond total returns derivable from the long-rate column.
what_we_took: Nothing yet (the 2026-07-18 long-horizon replay used French+GS10 from 1953; Shiller extends it to 1871).
what_we_rejected: Using it as vintage/PIT data — it is a modern reconstruction; safe as return history, not as what-was-knowable-when for earnings.
still_unmined: Extend long_horizon_mandates.py to 1871 (captures 1929) via Shiller long-rate bond TRs.

name: Damodaran historical returns (NYU)
what_it_does_well: Annual 1928-2025 returns for S&P, small-cap, T-bill, 10Y T-bond, Baa corporate, real estate, gold in one xls; updated Jan 2026.
what_we_took: Nothing yet — cross-check anchor for the long-horizon mandate replay.
still_unmined: Use as independent validation of the French/GS10-based replay numbers.

name: SEC Form N-PORT structured datasets
what_it_does_well: Quarterly ZIPs of structured monthly mutual-fund/ETF holdings 2019q4+ — full portfolios incl. bonds/derivatives, beyond 13F's >$100M institutions. Filing/acceptance dates in-data.
what_we_took: Nothing yet (N-PORT was already flagged in DATA_OPTIONS; the structured bulk datasets are the new find — no XML parsing needed).
still_unmined: T12 N-PORT collector via the bulk datasets; PIT caveat to verify at build: only month-3 reports become public, ~60d lag — observed_at = dissemination date.

name: SEC Schedule 13D/13G (activist stakes)
what_it_does_well: >5% activist stake events back decades; EDGAR acceptance-datetime = PIT-gold; new 13D deadline is 5 business days (short, knowable lag).
what_we_took: Nothing yet — needs only a new form-type filter on the existing _sec_get plumbing, no new fetcher.
still_unmined: Activist-stake event collector (descriptive first; forward-IC only via pre-registration).

name: FINRA equity short interest files
what_it_does_well: Bi-monthly short-interest CSVs, archive to 2014 (regime break Jun 2021: OTC-only before, exchange-listed after). Settlement date + published dissemination schedule (~T+8) = PIT-safe.
what_we_took: Nothing yet (app currently shows short interest via yfinance snapshots — no history).
still_unmined: Historical short-interest panel + forward collector; flag the 2021 regime break and the non-commercial license in provenance.

name: SEC fails-to-deliver (CNS)
what_it_does_well: Twice-monthly FTD zips per security, verified back to 2009 on the current page (FOIA archive claims 2004+); squeeze/stress microstructure with 20+ years of history.
what_we_took: Nothing yet.
still_unmined: FTD stress candidate for the fragility composite (descriptive); encode the ~1-month publication lag as observed_at.

name: CBOE put/call ratio archives
what_it_does_well: Free daily total/index/equity put-call archives 1995→2019-10 (verified: the free daily archive STOPS 2019-10-04; post-2019 daily is paid DataShop or forward-scraping the daily stats page).
what_we_took: Nothing yet (options_intelligence computes current P/C from chains; no history).
still_unmined: 24yr backfill for the fragility composite's P/C candidate + forward collector for the daily stats page.

name: JST Macrohistory Database (macrohistory.net)
what_it_does_well: 1870→present, 18 countries, 48 variables including equity/housing/bond/bill RETURNS — the best free cross-country 150-year return panel in existence. Annual only.
what_we_took: Nothing yet.
what_we_rejected: Vendoring the data into the repo without handling BY-NC-SA share-alike/attribution.
still_unmined: Cross-country robustness check of the mandate replay (does 40/50/10 survive in Japan/Germany/UK?) — offline research, cite with attribution.

name: EDGAR full-text search API (efts.sec.gov)
what_it_does_well: Keyless JSON API over all filings + exhibits, verified hits back to 2001; acceptance datetime per hit = PIT-gold. The durable free path to fundamental TEXT history (8-K exhibits, press releases, shareholder letters).
what_we_took: Nothing yet — routes through the existing _sec_get limiter when built.
still_unmined: The 8-K/risk-factor/guidance text-mining program (DATA_OPTIONS #1 free recommendation) now has its search backbone.

name: Robintrack archive (2018-2020 retail holdings)
what_it_does_well: Hourly per-ticker Robinhood holder counts 2018-05→2020-08 (verified downloadable, 529MB) — a unique frozen retail-behavior panel spanning the COVID crash, hourly-stamped (PIT-fine).
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: A collector — the panel can never accrue forward (Robinhood killed the API 2020); research-only if ever used.
still_unmined: One-off research: did retail holder-count spikes lead/lag the COVID drawdown/recovery? (descriptive curiosity, low priority)

name: WhaleWisdom
what_it_does_well: 13F aggregation with 2001+ history and rankings.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: ~$300/yr for what the wired EDGAR 13F pipeline already ingests from source; free/API tier limited to trailing 8 quarters.

name: Dataroma
what_it_does_well: 84 superinvestor portfolios, quarterly — a curated repackaging of 13F.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: No API, all-rights-reserved, and it is derived from 13Fs we already ingest from source; at most a UI-pattern reference for presenting famous-investor holdings.

name: ICI mutual fund flows
what_it_does_well: Weekly estimated US fund flows in free per-year xls files.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Deep weekly history not confirmed free; terms unverified — borderline.
still_unmined: Revisit only if fund-flow data becomes a pre-registered trial input.

name: ApeWisdom (WSB sentiment API)
what_it_does_well: Live free API for Reddit ticker-mention rankings.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: No historical depth, no snapshot timestamps documented — current-rankings only; weak vs the GDELT + Trends attention proxies already wired.

name: Motley Fool earnings-call transcripts
what_it_does_well: Free-to-read transcript library.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: No API/bulk, scrape-fragile, no redistribution rights — EDGAR full-text search is the durable free text path instead.

name: MeasuringWorth
what_it_does_well: Extraordinary depth (gold to 1257, US rates to 1729) — mostly annual.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: Overlaps Shiller/Damodaran for our needs; contact-for-other-uses license — cite-only.

name: Bank of England millennium dataset
what_it_does_well: A millennium of UK macro data in one file.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: UK-centric and annual; superseded for our mandate work by JST (which includes the UK).

name: Hedge-fund letter aggregators (FinMasters, GuruFocus, BuySide Digest et al.)
what_it_does_well: Curated links to investor letters.
what_we_took: Nothing.
what_we_rejected: No structured data, no redistribution rights, shallow archives; famous-investor POSITIONS are better served by the wired 13F pipeline.

name: datahub.io gold-prices (monthly LBMA)
what_it_does_well: Long monthly gold price CSV (1950+), simple direct URL — filled the gap left by FRED discontinuing its LBMA series in 2022.
what_we_took: Adopted 2026-07-18 as the alt-sleeve series in engine/research/long_horizon_mandates.py (research-only, yfinance GC=F fallback).
